04/09/2026 04/09/2026 Patient is here for a preop visit. Patient is scheduled for
ACDF at C4-5 and C5-6 on 4/15/2026 at BUMCP.
Current Pain med: Naproxen prn (already stopped)
**Patient is on ozempic, last injection on 4/4 and holding**
I reviewed patients most recent x-ray and MRI and agree with
above surgical plan. Patient was shown the nature of the
surgery on their own imaging as well as computer generated
models. We again reviewed the surgery details, including
risks and benefits. We discussed potential complications
including, not limited to, bleeding, infection, reaction to
anesthesia, dysphagia, dysphonia, recurrent laryngeal nerve
palsy, persistent pain and need for additional intervention. We
expectations, and discharge planning. Medications and
allergies have been reviewed. We have reviewed the
preoperative instruction sheet and I have instructed the
patient to read the spine passport or have a care provider
read through the spine passport prior to surgery and keep in
an easily accessible location. Patient is aware no opiate
medications will be provided beyond 6 weeks from surgical
date, and it is recommended to take a stool softener while
Today patient is having progressive weakness in the bilateral
upper and lower extremities. Given worsening symptoms I
recommended that we move surgery to Saturday 4/11 at
CISH. I discussed this with Dr. Chutkan and he is in
agreement that we need to proceed with surgery sooner due
Patient has completed preoperative clearances which are
scanned into chart. We have ensured the patient is medically
fit for surgery by review of preop clearance records.
All questions have been answered to patient satisfaction and
patient is clear to proceed with surgery at this time. Patient
understand that they will be n.p.o at midnight prior to surgery.
They will await phone call (day prior to surgery) with check in
I spent at least 60 minutes on today's encounter the patient
between review of documentation, face to face time, as well
as documentation after. This included discussion with Dr.
Chutkan, surgical team and our surgery scheduler.

Patient is here for a preop visit. Patient is scheduled for ACDF at C4-5 and C5-6 on 4/15/2026 at BUMCP.
Notes: General Appearance: General appearance: healthy appearing and no acute distress; Alert and oriented
appropriately Mental status: oriented to person, place, and time. Mood and affect: normal affect. Skin: Cervical
spine: normal. Neurological System: Right upper extremity sensation (location): normal. Left upper extremity
sensation (location): normal. Right lower extremity sensation (location): normal. Left lower extremity sensation
(location): normal. Right upper extremity motor function (muscle group): biceps 4/5 grip strength 4/5 and
interossei 4/5 and deltoid 5/5, wrist extensors 5/5, and triceps 5/5. Left upper extremity motor function (muscle
group): biceps 4/5 triceps 4/5 grip strength 4/5 , and interossei 4/5 and deltoid 5/5 and wrist extensors 5/5. Right
lower extremity motor function (muscle group): hip flexors 5/5, quadriceps 5/5, hamstrings 5/5, anterior tibial 5/5, EHL
5/5, and gastrocnemius 5/5. Left lower extremity motor function (muscle group): hip flexors 3/5 quadriceps 5/5,
hamstrings 5/5, anterior tibial 5/5, EHL 5/5, and gastrocnemius 5/5. Right lower extremity reflexes: patellar 2/4. Left
lower extremity reflexes: patellar 2/4. Right sided tests: Hoffman's negative and no clonus on ankle dorsiflexion. Left
sided tests: Hoffman's positive and no clonus on ankle dorsiflexion. Gait: Gait: normal. Tandem gait test: unable
Cervical Spine: Sagittal balance: normal. Midline cervical tenderness: none. Right cervical tenderness none. Left
cervical tenderness none. Cervical motion: normal motion and no pain with motion. Right sided cervical tests:
cervical facet loading negative and Spurling's negative. Left sided cervical tests: cervical facet loading negative and
